title: rosa villosa var. mollis
intro: Rosa villosa var. mollis, also known as the velvet rose or hairy rose, is a perennial shrub native to North America, characterized by its soft, velvety leaves and fragrant pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rosa villosa var. mollis include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of digestive issues. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected area.
